title: Acourtia umbratalis
taxonomy: Acourtia umbratalis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification indicates its relationship to other plants within this family, such as daisies and sunflowers. The genus Acourtia is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. Acourtia umbratalis is a specific species within this genus and family. Its classification is used in botanical nomenclature to identify and distinguish it from other plant species.
uses: Acourtia umbratalis is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its leaves and stems are often employed to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and potential interactions with other treatments.

intro: Acourtia umbratalis, also known as Chupaderito, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Acourtia umbratalis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Acourtia umbratalis is commonly used in traditional medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
